summary: Joel announces that FogBugz 5.0.31 now ships with German language support, making it the product's first multi-lingual version. Rather than creating a separate German edition, both languages are built into the default product, with per-user language selection that defaults to browser settings. The team uses a Pig Latin translation step to ensure all strings are localizable before handing them to German translators. Fog Creek has also established sales and support operations in Munich.
key_points: FogBugz ships its first multi-lingual version supporting English and German, with language selection per user or auto-detected from browser settings
Rather than maintaining separate product editions for different languages, localization is built into the single default product
The team uses Pig Latin as an intermediate translation step to verify all strings are properly localizable before real translation
Fog Creek established local sales and support in Munich with German-language customer service
money_quotes: Rather than have a different edition of FogBugz, the default edition now just supports both languages.
To ensure that every string is localizable, our developers translate everything to Pig Latin first before handing it off to the German translators.
key_insight: Building localization into the default product rather than maintaining separate editions, and using a pseudo-translation step like Pig Latin to catch unlocalizable strings, is a practical approach to internationalization.
